title: The Growth of E-Commerce
description: For questions 17 – 24, read the text below. Use the word given in capitals at the end of some of the lines to form a word that fits in the gap in the same line. There is an example at the beginning (0).

content.text: The rapid (0) expansion of e-commerce over the past two decades has transformed the way people shop. Online (17) retailers now offer a vast range of products at (18) competitive prices, making it easy for consumers to compare options and find what they need. The (19) convenience of shopping from home has led millions of people to abandon traditional high streets.||Despite its many (20) advantages, e-commerce has also raised concerns about (21) employment in the retail sector, as many physical stores have closed. There are also growing worries about (22) environmental impacts, since increased delivery traffic contributes to carbon emissions. Companies are now under (23) considerable pressure to find more (24) sustainable solutions, such as electric delivery vehicles and reduced packaging.

example.text: The rapid (0) EXPANSION of e-commerce over the past two decades has transformed the way people shop. (EXPAND)

questions.explanation: The plural noun 'retailers' is formed from 'retail' by adding '-er' and '-s'.
The adjective 'competitive' is formed from 'compete' via 'competition' + '-ive'.
The noun 'convenience' is formed from 'convenient' by replacing '-ent' with '-ence'.
The plural noun 'advantages' is formed from 'advantage' by adding '-s'.
The noun 'employment' is formed from 'employ' by adding the suffix '-ment'.
The adjective 'environmental' is formed from 'environment' by adding the suffix '-al'.
The adjective 'considerable' is formed from 'consider' by adding the suffix '-able'.
The adjective 'sustainable' is formed from 'sustain' by adding the suffix '-able'.
